metadata.notes: agent_supports: yes (kiro auth IS a subscription) but NOTHING subscription/credit-related reaches the tailed .jsonl and the kiro adapter has no statusline/rate-limit/quota integration в†’ daemon=incapable; route frozen

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: incapable; driver_capability: ready (moot)
в†’ route: frozen

## The scenario's assertion

The user-observable signal is whether the daemon surfaces a subscription / billing-model reading per session: a non-nil `RateLimitSnapshot` (with в‰Ґ1 `Window`, or a non-empty `PlanType` / non-nil `Credits` for the codex variant) for a subscription session, vs. a nil snapshot for an API-key session. Two sibling adapters record this row, each via a DIFFERENT daemon surface:

- **claudecode = full** вЂ” the subscription `rate_limits` block arrives OUT-OF-BAND via the statusline HTTP hook (`POST /api/v1/hooks/claudecode/statusline`); `statuslineToSnapshot()` turns it into a `RateLimitSnapshot`, nil when the block is absent (API-key path). (statusline.go:99-145.)
- **codex = full** вЂ” the `rate_limits` map (+ `plan_type` / `credits`) arrives IN-BAND inside the JSONL `token_count` event; `extractCodexRateLimits()` reads it straight off the tailed transcript (codex/parser.go:96, 374-423). No hook needed.

So irrlicht has TWO working transports for this signal вЂ” an HTTP statusline receiver and an in-band JSONL rate_limits field вЂ” but BOTH are adapter-specific, and kiro-cli wires NEITHER.

## Why agent=yes

Kiro's auth model IS a subscription. The user runs `kiro-cli login` and authenticates with a Google / AWS Builder ID; `kiro-cli whoami` prints the authenticated subscription identity; `/stats` and the TUI footer render per-turn credits (`в–ё Credits: 0.03 вЂў Time: 1s`); models carry credit-rate multipliers; the sidecar `<uuid>.json` carries a per-turn `metering_usage` credit array and a `context_usage_percentage` (FINDINGS.md В§4,В§6). The agent unambiguously operates on a subscription and exposes subscription/credit state to the user. By the higher-bar rule (the surface need not be a top-level flag вЂ” REPL/footer state counts), agent=yes is honest. This is the load-bearing difference from the kiro `token-quota-exhausted` sibling, which is agent=no (kiro performs no observable exhaustion behavior at all).

## Why daemon=incapable (the load-bearing verdict в†’ frozen)

The block is entirely on the daemon pillar, and it is architectural, not a fixable bug. Two independent reasons, each cited:

1. **The signal never reaches the Source the daemon tails.** Kiro's Source is `FilesUnderRoot{Dir: .kiro/sessions/cli, JSONLineParser}` (agent.go:38-43) вЂ” the daemon tails ONLY the `.jsonl`. That JSONL's entire 2.5.1 vocabulary is `Prompt / AssistantMessage / ToolResults / Clear`, with NO model, token, cost, credit, plan, or rate-limit field (FINDINGS.md В§4; parser.go:21 documents this in a comment). The subscription/credit accounting lives ONLY in the `<uuid>.json` sidecar's `metering_usage`, which the daemon does not read. There is no event in `events.jsonl` that could prove a per-session subscription-vs-API-key reading, because no such field is in the tailed transport.

2. **The kiro adapter has no quota/statusline/capacity integration.** A repo-wide grep of `core/adapters/inbound/agents/kirocli/` for `statusline|ratelimit|rate_limit|IngestRateLimit|quota|credit|capacity|PlanType` matches exactly ONE line вЂ” adapter.go:18, a passing comment that the sidecar carries per-turn credits вЂ” and zero handlers. There is no statusline HTTP receiver registered for kiro (the statusline hook path is claudecode-only, hardwired to the `claudecode/statusline` route + the claudecode `transcript_path` shape), and the kiro parser emits only `user_message / assistant / turn_done / tool_result`, never a `RateLimit`-bearing event. The kiro adapter registers a SINGLE permission, `transcripts` (agent.go:44-57) вЂ” no quota/statusline permission exists for it.

Neither claudecode's nor codex's transport applies: kiro emits no in-band `rate_limits` JSONL field (unlike codex), and kiro's CLI is not configured/able to POST a statusline payload to the daemon (unlike claudecode's `statusLine.command` hook). The 3-state model + the tailed JSONL simply cannot represent a subscription reading for kiro. That is `daemon=incapable` вЂ” no trace exists in the Source вЂ” not `daemon=bug` (no mis-handled trace) and not a driver gap (no recipe step is missing; the signal itself is absent).

## driver вЂ” moot under daemon=incapable

The TUI driver is real and could drive a turn (interactive driver exists; FINDINGS.md В§6-8), so the driver pillar is `ready`, but it is moot: there is no observation to elicit. No recipe would make the absent signal appear.

## Observations (no model/token/cost in the JSONL)

The kiro JSONL carries no model/token/cost (parser.go:21), so the cell's observation vector is model=`unknown`, cost_nonzero=false, tokens_nonzero=false. (Moot for a frozen cell вЂ” no recording is produced вЂ” but recorded for consistency with the other kiro cells.)

## Disposition

Frozen. Kiro genuinely runs on a subscription (agent=yes), but no subscription/credit/rate-limit signal reaches the JSONL the daemon tails and the kiro adapter wires neither of irrlicht's two existing transports (statusline hook / in-band rate_limits), so irrlicht fundamentally cannot observe a per-session subscription reading for kiro today. Re-assessable only if (a) a future kiro release writes a rate-limit/plan/credit field into the tailed `.jsonl`, OR (b) a kiro statusline-style POST hook is added, AND the kiro adapter grows the matching ingestion (e.g. a sidecar MetricsReader reading `metering_usage`, the documented future enhancement, plus a snapshot mapping).
caveats: agent=yes for the RIGHT reason: kiro's auth IS a subscription (kiro-cli login / Google/AWS Builder ID, kiro-cli whoami, /stats + TUI footer credits) вЂ” but NONE of that reaches the daemon. The block is purely daemon=incapable, not agent.

Distinct from the kiro token-quota-exhausted sibling: that cell is agent=no (no exhaustion behavior at all); this one is agent=yes/daemon=incapable (the behavior exists but is invisible to the tailed transport).

irrlicht HAS two working transports for this signal (claudecode's out-of-band statusline hook; codex's in-band JSONL rate_limits field) вЂ” kiro wires NEITHER. This is architecture (incapable), not a mis-handled trace (bug) and not a missing driver step (gap).

Subscription/credit accounting lives only in the <uuid>.json sidecar (metering_usage, context_usage_percentage); the daemon does not read it. A sidecar MetricsReader is a documented future enhancement and would still need a snapshot mapping before this row could record.
model=unknown, cost_nonzero=false, tokens_nonzero=false: the kiro JSONL carries no model/token/cost (moot вЂ” frozen, no recording produced).
Re-assess only if a future kiro release surfaces a rate-limit/plan/credit field in the tailed .jsonl (or a kiro statusline POST hook lands) AND the kiro adapter adds matching ingestion.

sources.note: kiro 2.5.1 JSONL vocabulary = Prompt/AssistantMessage/ToolResults/Clear; NO model/token/cost/credit/rate-limit. Subscription/credit data lives only in the <uuid>.json sidecar (per-turn metering_usage credits + context_usage_percentage; input/output_token_count are 0). Credits render in /stats and the TUI footer вЂ” never in the tailed transport.

Source = FilesUnderRoot{Dir: .kiro/sessions/cli, JSONLineParser} вЂ” the daemon tails ONLY the JSONL (no rate-limit/credit trace). The adapter registers a SINGLE permission, 'transcripts'; there is no quota/statusline permission or handler for kiro.

Parser emits only user_message/assistant/turn_done/tool_result; the comment states the JSONL carries no model/token/cost and the parser never reads the sidecar. No RateLimit-bearing event is ever produced.

Exactly ONE match вЂ” adapter.go:18, a passing comment that the sidecar carries per-turn credits вЂ” and ZERO handlers. The kiro adapter wires no statusline receiver, no rate-limit ingestion, no quota/capacity integration.

Reference transport #1 (claudecode=full): the subscription rate_limits block arrives OUT-OF-BAND via POST /api/v1/hooks/claudecode/statusline; statuslineToSnapshot() builds a RateLimitSnapshot (nil when the block is absent). This route is claudecode-specific (hardwired route + claudecode transcript_path shape); kiro has no equivalent POST hook.

Reference transport #2 (codex=full): the rate_limits map (+ plan_type / credits) arrives IN-BAND inside the JSONL token_count event; extractCodexRateLimits() reads it off the tailed transcript. Kiro's JSONL has no such field, so this in-band path also does not apply.

Sibling kiro cell вЂ” agent=no there (kiro performs no observable quota-exhaustion behavior). This subscription-detection cell differs on the AGENT pillar (agent=yes: kiro genuinely runs on a subscription); both share daemon=incapable for the same reason вЂ” nothing quota/credit-related reaches the tailed .jsonl.

recipe.notes: Not recordable: frozen on daemon=incapable. Kiro's auth IS a subscription (kiro-cli login / Builder ID, kiro-cli whoami, /stats + TUI footer credits вЂ” agent=yes), but NONE of that subscription/credit/rate-limit state reaches the tailed .jsonl (vocabulary = Prompt/AssistantMessage/ToolResults/Clear; no model/token/cost/credit; the data lives only in the <uuid>.json sidecar's metering_usage, which the daemon does not read). The kiro adapter wires NEITHER of irrlicht's two existing transports for this signal вЂ” claudecode's out-of-band statusline POST hook nor codex's in-band JSONL rate_limits field вЂ” so no RateLimitSnapshot can ever be produced for a kiro session (grep of the adapter for statusline/rate_limit/quota/credit/capacity matches only one comment, zero handlers). The daemon fundamentally cannot observe a per-session subscription reading for kiro; driver is moot. Re-assess only if a future kiro release writes a rate-limit/plan/credit field into the tailed .jsonl (or a kiro statusline POST hook lands) AND the kiro adapter grows matching ingestion (e.g. a sidecar MetricsReader over metering_usage + a snapshot mapping). See assessment.json.
